🇷🇺 ☦️ Junior Lieutenant Nikolai Sergeevich Sharapov (“Veles”), 1994–2026. A war correspondent, special forces reconnaissance officer, and UAV operator. He took part in the conflict from 2022, serving in reconnaissance units of the 2nd Army, “Center” group, operating on some of the most intense sectors of the front. He commanded a reconnaissance group, worked with drones, and was known among his comrades as a disciplined, capable, and steady officer. He also wrote the book “Through My Own Eyes” about the war in Ukraine, which later received the Arsenyev Literary Prize (2024). Fell in action while performing his duty. Known to friends and colleagues as “Veles” — a professional soldier, writer, and man who stayed in the fight until the end. Eternal glory to the hero! 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇮🇷 The Israeli Air Force announced that they have conducted a large‑scale strike overnight against dozens of Iranian Air Force and IRGC Air Force aircraft and helicopters at three airports in Tehran: Bahram, Mehrabad and Azmayesh. Mehrabad Airport, targeted multiple times during Operation Roaring Lion, was used by the Quds Force as a hub for arming and financing the "regime’s proxies" across the Middle East. The IDF said it will continue to degrade the core capabilities of the "Iranian regime." @wfwitness
New footage released by Iranian police shows firing with heavy guns at U.S. helicopters trying to rescue F-15E crew members in Iran.

Israel will supply Greece with rocket launchers and munitions in a €650 million deal. The agreement, signed in Athens, includes Elbit Systems Ltd’s PULS rocket artillery system. The package includes launchers, training rockets, precision-guided missiles, and loitering munitions.

Open Source Intel Video shows a U.S. Air Force C-295W from the 427th Special Operations Squadron flying low over the western Iranian desert. The aircraft was part of the exfiltration effort for special forces operating behind enemy lines during the rescue mission. The C-295W is a rare special operations platform used by the 427th SOS under AFSOC. Only a small number are in service. Built by Airbus in Spain, it is designed for short and unprepared runways, low level flight, and insertion and extraction missions in austere environments. tweet

Senator Graham: It must be clear that when this war is over - either via diplomacy or military action - that control of the Strait of Hormuz does not lie with Iran. This war must end in a fashion — either through diplomacy or military action — that prevents Iran from going back to their old ways.

Chinese AI firms are using satellite data + open-source intel to map U.S. military assets, and that data may be helping Iran improve targeting. Companies like MizarVision publish AI-enhanced maps of U.S. bases, aircraft, and ships. Data comes from commercial satellites + public tracking tools. U.S. intel believes Iran (IRGC) may use it to plan missile/drone strikes more precisely. Source: ABC Exclusive

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇬🇷 The Israeli and Greek defence ministries have signed a major export deal for the supply of PULS rocket artillery systems to the Greek army, worth approximately 2.3 billion shekels (about 650 million euros). The system, manufactured by Elbit Systems, includes launchers and a comprehensive package of guided rockets. The agreement includes industrial cooperation, with production of some components in Greece. Implementation will take place over four years, followed by a ten‑year support period. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 🇮🇱 Hezbollah claimed multiple attacks on Monday, including rocket barrages on Nahariya, Kiryat Shmona, Metulla and Ma'alot Tarshiha, as well as strikes on Israeli military gatherings at the Fatima Gate crossing, Ibl al-Qam, and several locations in Ainata (hills of Sedr, Ghamatha and Freiz). The group also claimed a direct hit on a Merkava tank in Rashaf and said it targeted the Givat Olga base, which belongs to the IDF Northern Command, 75 kilometres from the border, using a specialised missile and attack drones. Additionally, Hezbollah said it shot down an Israeli Hermes 450 drone over Ainata with a surface‑to‑air missile. @wfwitness

Yemen’s Houthis say they will honor a ceasefire with the United States as long as Washington stops attacks against them. A senior Houthi official, Mohammed Ali al-Houthi, said the group has no plans to target Red Sea shipping or US assets unless the conflict escalates. He added they will not attack other Muslim countries unless provoked. However, they warned that if tensions rise, they could close the strategic Bab al-Mandab Strait. They also said Saudi Red Sea ports will not be targeted as long as Saudi Arabia avoids involvement in escalation. Source: CNN
